[[Category:Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Creation myths]] [[Category:Kojiki]] [[Category:Qq]][[lld:Kamiumi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ja:%E7%A5%9E%E7%94%A3%E3%81%BF]] [[Category:Kamiumi forks]]

'''Ōya-biko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]] that appears in [[Japanese mythology]]. There are two deities with the same name.
* One of the {{ill|Katakurokushin|ja|家宅六神}}. It is considered to be the same deity as {{ill|Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神}}. * An alternative name for {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神}}.
{{aimai}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōya-biko-no-Kami}} {{Shinto-stub}}
# y [[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:大屋毘古神]] # {{ill|KazamoTsuwake-noOshiO|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:風木津別之忍男神]], male deity # {{ill|ŌwataTsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:大綿津見神]], genderless deity and spirit # {{ill|HayaAkitsuHiKo|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:速秋津日子神]], male deity # {{ill|HayaAkitsuHiMe|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:速秋津比売神]], female deity
:From the relationship between HayaAkitsuHiKo and HayaAkitsuHiMe the following gods were born:<ref name="kojiki-p77-78">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|pp=77–78]]</ref> ## {{ill|AwaNagi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:沫那藝神]], male deity ## {{ill|AwaNami|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:沫那美神]], female deity ## {{ill|TsuraNagi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:頬那藝神]], male deity ## {{ill|TsuraNami|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:頬那美神]], female deity ## {{ill|Ame-noMikumari|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:天之水分神]], genderless deity and spirit ## {{ill|Kuni-noMikumari|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:国之水分神]], genderless deity and spirit ## {{ill|Ame-noKuhizamochi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:天之久比奢母智神]], genderless deity and spirit ## {{ill|Kuni-noKuhizamochi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:国之久比奢母智神]], genderless deity and spirit
# {{ill|ShinatsuHiko|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:志那都比古神]], male deity # {{ill|Kukunochi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:久久能智神]], genderless deity and spirit # {{ill|OhoyamaTsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:大山津見神]], male deity # {{ill|Kaya-noHiMe|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:鹿屋野比売神]], also known as {{ill|NoZuchi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:野椎神]], female deity
:From the relationship between OhoyamaTsumi and Kaya-noHiMe the following gods were born:<ref name="kojiki-p77-78"/> ## {{ill|Ame-noSazuchi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:天之狭土神]], genderless deity and spirit ## {{ill|Kuni-noSazuchi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:国之狭土神]], genderless deity and spirit ## {{ill|Ame-noSagiri|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:天之狭霧神]], genderless deity and spirit ## {{ill|Kuni-noSagiri|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:国之狭霧神]], genderless deity and spirit ## {{ill|Ame-noKurado|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:天之闇戸神]], genderless deity and spirit ## {{ill|Kuni-noKurado|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:国之闇戸神]], genderless deity and spirit ## {{ill|OhotoMatoHiKo|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:大戸惑子神]], male deity ## {{ill|OhotoMatoHiMe|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:大戸惑女神]], female deity
# {{ill|Tori-noIwakusuFune|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:鳥之石楠船神]], also known as {{ill|Ame-noToriFune|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:天鳥船]], genderless deity and spirit # {{ill|UkeMochi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:大宜都比売神]], female deity, Goddess of food # {{ill|Hi-noYagiHayaO|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:火之夜藝速男神]] = ''{{ill|KaguTsuchi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:火之迦具土神]]'', also known as {{ill|Hi-noKagaBiko|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:火之炫毘古神]] and {{ill|Hi-noKaguTsuchi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:火之迦具土神]], male deity, Kami of fire
=== Gods Born from Izanami’s Agony === During ''KaguTsuchi''’s birth, Izanami's genitals were burned, and she was mortally wounded. In her agony, from her vomit, urine, and feces more gods were born:<ref name="kojiki-p79"/>
# {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:金山彦神|lt=Kanayamahiko]], male deity born from Izanami's vomit and feces # {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:金山毘売神|lt=Kanayamahime]], female deity born from Izanami's vomit and feces # {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:ハニヤスビコ|lt=Haniyasu-hiko]], male deity born from Izanami's feces # {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:波邇夜須毘売神|lt=Haniyasu-hime]], female deity born from Izanami's feces # {{ill|Mizuhanome|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:ミヅハノメ]], female deity born from Izanami's urine # {{ill|Wakumusubi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:ワクムスビ]], male deity born from Izanami's urine ## WakuMuSubi had a daughter: ## {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:トヨウケビメ]], goddess of agriculture, female deity
===Death of Kagutsuchi=== After the agony, Izanami dies. At the time Izanagi crept moaning about the body and mourned her death. From his tears, the female deity {{ill|Naki-sawame|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:泣沢女神]] was born. Subsequently, Izanagi buried Izanami on {{ill|Mount Hiba|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:比婆山]]. His sadness turned into anger, and he decided to kill Kagutsuchi with a ten-grasp<ref group="nb" name="grasp">A "grasp" is the breadth of four fingers when the hand is clenched.</ref> sword called '''Ame-no-Oha-bari''' (archaic name) or '''Ame-no-Woha-bari''' {{ill|Itsu-no-Oha-bari|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:天之尾羽張|lt=also known as Itsu-no-Oha-bari]].<ref name="kojiki-p79">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=79]]</ref><ref name="kojiki-p80-81">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|pp=80–81]]</ref>
From the blood of Kagutsuchi, the following gods emerged:<ref name="kojiki-p80-81"/>
# {{ill|Iwa-saku|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:石折神]]<ref group="nb">Chamberlain: Ihasaku</ref> # {{ill|Ne-saku|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:根折神]] - Minor Star God. # {{ill|Iwa-tsutsu-no-O|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:石筒之男神]] #: The gods above were born from the blood that fell from the tip of the sword on the rocks. # {{ill|Mika-Hayahi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:甕速日神]] # {{ill|Hi-Hayahi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:樋速日神]] # {{ill|Take-Mikagu-tsuchi-no-O|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:建御雷之男神]], also known as {{ill|Takefutsu|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:建布都神]] or {{ill|Toyo-futsu|en}} #: The gods above were born from the blood that fell from the blade of the sword. # {{ill|Kura-Okami|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:闇淤加美神]] # {{ill|Kura-Mitsuha|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:闇御津羽神]] #: The gods above were born from the blood that fell from the handle of the sword.
Also, from the body of Kagutsuchi, the following gods were born:<ref name="kojiki-p80-81"/> # {{ill|Masaka-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:正鹿山津見神]], emerged from Kagutsuchi's head; # {{ill|Odo-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:淤縢山津見神]], from the chest; # {{ill|Oku-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:奥山津見神]], from the abdomen; # {{ill|Kura-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:闇山津見神]] (or {{ill|Kura-yama-gi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:闇山祇]]), from the genitals; # {{ill|Shigi-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:志藝山津見神]], from the left arm; # {{ill|Ha-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:羽山津見神]], from the right arm; # {{ill|Hara-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:原山津見神]], from the left foot; # {{ill|Toya-ma-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:戸山津見神]] (or {{ill|Toya-ma-gi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:戸山祇]]), from the right foot.
===Land of Yomi=== [[File:Green willow and other Japanese fairy tales (1910) (14593788578).jpg|thumb]] Izanagi then decided to bring back Izanami and goes to [[Yomi|Yomi-no-kuni]], the underworld. Crossing the gates to that world, he met Izanami and says to her:<ref name="kojiki-p82-83">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|pp=82–83]]</ref>
{{blockquote|The countries that you and me have created have not been completed yet. Let us return!<ref group="nb">「あなたと一緒に創った国土はまだ完成していません。帰りましょう。」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
Izanami replied:
{{blockquote|Too bad you did not come before, for I have eaten in the country of Yomi! [By eating food in the land of Yomi, one ends up stuck being a resident of Yomi. This concept is called, "Yomotsu Hegui (黄泉戸喫)."]<ref>戸部民夫 『日本神話』 29-30頁。</ref> ... however, I will consult with the gods of Yomi. Under no circumstances you may look at me!<ref group="nb">「黄泉の国の食べ物を食べてしまったので、生き返ることはできません」「黄泉神と相談しましょう。お願いですから、私の姿は見ないで下さいね。」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
On saying this, Izanami entered the palace of these gods. However, time passed and she did not return and Izanagi began to despair. So he broke one of the tines of his ornamental comb mizura that he wore in the left bun of his hair, lit it in order to light the place and decided to enter the world of dead. He manages to find Izanami but is surprised to see that she lost her beauty and had become a rotting corpse, covered with maggots. Of her body were born the eight Gods of thunder, which were:<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
# {{nihongo|'''[[Ō-ikazuchi]]'''|大雷]], from the head of Izanami; # {{nihongo|'''[[Hono-ikazuchi]]'''|火雷]], from her chest; # {{nihongo|'''[[Kuro-ikazuchi]]'''|黒雷]], from her abdomen; # {{nihongo|'''[[Saku-ikazuchi]]'''|折雷]], from her genitals; # {{nihongo|'''[[Waka-ikazuchi]]'''|若雷]], from her left arm; # {{nihongo|'''[[Tsuchi-ikazuchi]]'''|土雷]], from her right arm; # {{nihongo|'''[[Naru-ikazuchi]]'''|鳴雷]], from her left foot; # {{nihongo|'''[[Fusu-ikazuchi]]'''|伏雷]], from her right foot.
Izanagi, shocked, decided to return home, but Izanami was embarrassed by his appearance and commanded the {{nihongo|Yomotsu-shikome|黄泉 丑女|lit. "horrible women from the world of darkness"]] to chase Izanagi. In his flight, he took the head-dress from his head, and threw it to the ground where it turned into a [[bunch of grapes]]. The Yomotsushikome started to eat them but kept chasing the fleeing Izanagi. So he broke the tine of the comb that he wore in his right bun, and as he threw it to the ground it became bamboo shoots, prompting the Yomotsushikome to eat them and enabling Izanagi to flee.<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
However, Izanami decided to release the eight gods of thunder and 1,500 warriors from Yomi to continue the pursuit. Izanagi drew and brandished his [[Totsuka-no-Tsurugi]]<ref group="nb" name="grasp"/> sword to continue his flight. As they pursued him, Izanagi reached the {{nihongo|[[Yomotsu-hirasaka]]|黄泉比良坂]], the slope that descends from the land of the living to Yomi. He took three peaches from a tree that had grown in that place and threw them at his pursuers so that they fled.<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
Izanagi commented:
{{blockquote|Assistance to all people when they are tired and face difficulties.<ref group="nb">「人々が困っている時に助けてくれ」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
These peaches were called {{nihongo|Ōkamuzumi|意富加牟豆美命, Ōkamuzumi-no-mikoto]].<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
Finally, Izanami persecuted Izanagi, but he lifted a rock that a thousand men could not move and blocked the slope with it. At that moment, their eyes met for the last time.<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
Izanami said:
{{blockquote|If you behave in this way, I will strangle and kill one thousand men of your land in one day!<ref group="nb">「私はこれから毎日、一日に千人ずつ殺そう」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
Izanagi replied:
{{blockquote|If you do this, I will in one day set up 1,500 parturition houses. So in one day indeed 1,000 men will die and indeed 1,500 are going to be born.<ref group="nb">「それなら私は人間が決して滅びないよう、一日に千五百人生ませよう」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
These words justified the circle of life and death in humans. For the same reason, Izanami is also called {{nihongo|'''Yomotsu-ōkami'''|黄泉津大神]] or {{nihongo|'''Chishiki-no-ōkami'''|道敷大神]] and the boulder that covers the entrance to the world of the dead is known as {{nihongo|'''Chika'eshi-no-ōkami'''|道返之大神]] or {{nihongo|'''Yomido-no-ōkami'''|黄泉戸大神]] ō and is today known as {{nihongo|slope of Ifuya|伊賦夜坂, Ifuya-saka]] in [[Izumo, Shimane|Izumo]], [[Shimane Prefecture]].<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
===Purification of Izanagi=== [[File:Descendants of Izanagi alone-en.svg|thumb|300px|Gods that emerged during the purification of Izanagi.]] Leaving Yomi, Izanagi decided to remove all [[Kegare|uncleanness]] in his body through a purification ceremony (''[[misogi]]'') consisting of a bath in the river at Ahakihara in Tachibana no Ono in Tsukushi. As he stripped his clothes and accessories on the floor the following twelve gods are born:<ref name="kojiki-p84-85">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|pp=84–85]]</ref>
# [[Tsukitatsu funato]] (衝立船戸神 – Post at the Road Bend<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tsukitatsufunato • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/tsukitatsufunato/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>) = Chimata no Kami, emerges from the staff. # [[Michi no nagachiha]] (道之長乳歯神 – Long Winding Way Stones<ref>{{Cite web|title=Michinonagachiha • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/michinonagachiha/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the [[Obi (sash)|obi]]. # [[Tokihakashi]] (時量師神 – Time Keeper Loosed<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tokihakashi • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/tokihakashi/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the handbag. # [[Wazurai no ushi]] (和豆良比能宇斯能神 – Master Miasma<ref>{{Cite web|title=Wazurainoushi • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/wazurainoushi/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>),<ref group="nb">Chamberlain: Wazurahinoushi</ref> from cloths. # [[Michi mata]] (道俣神 – Road Fork<ref>{{Cite web|title=Chimata • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/chimata/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the [[hakama]]. # [[Akigui no ushi]] (飽咋之宇斯能神 – Master Filled Full<ref>{{Cite web|title=Akiguinoushi • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/akiguinoushi/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the crown [[Corona (disambiguation)|corona]]. # [[Oki zakaru]] (奥疎神 – Beyond Offshore<ref>{{Cite web|title=Okizakaru • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okizakaru/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the left hand. # [[Okitsu nagisa biko]] (奥津那芸佐毘古神 – Offshore Surf Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Okitsunagisabiko • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsunagisabiko/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the left hand. # [[Okitsu kaibera]] (奥津甲斐弁羅神 – Offshore Tide Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Okitsukaibera • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsukaibera/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>),<ref group="nb">Chamberlain: Okitsukahibera</ref> from the armband of the left hand. # [[He zakaru]] (辺疎神 – Beyond Shoreside<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hezakaru • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/hezakaru/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the right hand. # [[Hetsu nagisa biko]] (辺津那芸佐毘古神 – Shoreside Surf Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hetsunagisabiko • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/hetsunagisabiko/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the right hand. # [[Hetsu kaibera]] (辺津甲斐弁羅神 – Shoreside Tide Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hetsukaibera • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/hetsukaibera/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the right hand.
Subsequently Izanagi is stripped of impurities from the land of Yomi. In this moment two gods were born:
# {{ill|Yasomaga-Tsuhi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:八十禍津日神]], male deity # {{ill|Ōmaga-Tsuhi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:大禍津日神]], male deity
Then, shaking off the curse, three gods were born:
# {{ill|Kamuna-obi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:神直毘神]], male deity # {{ill|Ōna-obi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:大直毘神]], male deity # {{ill|Izunome (kami)|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:伊豆能売]], female deity
Then, when washing with water the lower parts of his body, two gods were born;
# {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Sokotsu-watatsumi]]'''|底津綿津見神]], genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sokotsutsuno'o]]'''|底筒之男神]], male deity
When washing the middle of his body, two more gods were born:
# {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Nakatsu-watatsumi]]'''|中津綿津見神]], genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Nakatsutsuno'o]]'''|中筒之男神]], male deity
Finally, washing the upper part of his body, two more gods were born:
# {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Uwatsu-watatsumi]]'''|上津綿津見神]],<ref group="nb">Chamberlain: Uhatsuwatatsumi</ref> genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Uwatsutsuno'o]]'''|上筒之男神]], male deity
The trio of Sokotsu-watatsumi, Nakatsu-watatsumi and Uwatsu-watatsumi make up the group of deities called [[Watatsumi|Sanjin Watatsumi]], or the gods of water. The trio of Sokotsutsuno'o, Nakatsutsuno'o and Uhatsutsuno'o make up the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] group of deities, gods of fishing and sea, to whom tribute is paid at [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]].<ref name="kojiki-p84-85"/>
In the last step of the purification ceremony, Izanagi washed his left eye from which the female deity {{nihongo|'''[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]'''|天照大御神]] was born; washed his right eye from which the genderless deity and spirit {{nihongo|'''[[Tsukuyomi|Tsukuyomi-no-mikoto]]'''|月読命]] was born; and when washing his nose from which the male deity {{nihongo|'''[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Takehaya-susano'o-no-mikoto]]'''|建速須佐之男命]] = commonly known as Susano'o was born.<ref name="kojiki-p84-85"/>
With these three gods called {{nihongo|[[Mihashira-no-uzu-no-miko]]|三貴子|extra=”Three precious children”]], Izanagi ordered their investiture. Amaterasu received the mandate to govern [[Takamagahara]] and a necklace of jewels called {{nihongo|Mikuratanano-no-kami|御倉板挙之神]] from Izanagi. Tsukuyomi is mandated to govern over the Dominion of the Night, and Takehaya-susano'o (建速須佐之男命) = Susano'o is to rule the seas.<ref name="kojiki-p86">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=86]]</ref>

'''Ōya-biko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]] that appears in [[Japanese mythology]]. There are two deities with the same name.
* One of the {{ill|Katakurokushin|ja|家宅六神}}. It is considered to be the same deity as {{ill|Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神}}. * An alternative name for {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神}}.

'''Ōya-biko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]][[ja: that appears in [[Japanese mythology]][[ja:. There are two deities with the same name.
* One of the {{ill|Katakurokushin|ja|家宅六神}}. It is considered to be the same deity as {{ill|Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神}}. * An alternative name for {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神}}.
{{aimai}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōya-biko-no-Kami}} {{Shinto-stub}}

'''Ōya-biko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. There are two deities with the same name.

'''Ōya-biko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. There are two deities with the same name.
* One of the {{ill|Katakurokushin|ja|家宅六神}}. It is considered to be the same deity as {{ill|Magatsuhi|lt=Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神}}. * An alternative name for {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神}}. '''Ōya-biko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. There are two deities with the same name.
* One of the {{ill|Katakurokushin|ja|家宅六神}}. It is considered to be the same deity as {{ill|Magatsuhi|lt=Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神}}. * An alternative name for {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神}}. '''Ōya-biko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. There are two deities with the same name.
* One of the {{ill|Katakurokushin|ja|家宅六神}}. It is considered to be the same deity as {{ill|Magatsuhi|lt=Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神}}. * An alternative name for {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神}}. '''Ōya-biko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. There are two deities with the same name.
* One of the {{ill|Katakurokushin|ja|家宅六神}}. It is considered to be the same deity as {{ill|Magatsuhi|lt=Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神}}. * An alternative name for {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神}}. '''Ōya-biko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. There are two deities with the same name.
* One of the {{ill|Katakurokushin|ja|家宅六神}}. It is considered to be the same deity as {{ill|Magatsuhi|lt=Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神}}. * An alternative name for {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神}}. '''Ōya-biko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. There are two deities with the same name.
* One of the {{ill|Katakurokushin|ja|家宅六神}}. It is considered to be the same deity as {{ill|Magatsuhi|lt=Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神}}. * An alternative name for {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神}}. revision.text:
'''Ōya-biko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. There are two deities with the same name.
* One of the {{ill|Katakurokushin|ja|家宅六神}}. It is considered to be the same deity as {{ill|Magatsuhi|lt=Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神}}. * An alternative name for {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神}}. '''Ōya-biko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. There are two deities with the same name.
* One of the {{ill|Katakurokushin|ja|家宅六神}}. It is considered to be the same deity as {{ill|Magatsuhi|lt=Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神}}. * An alternative name for {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神}}. '''Ōya-biko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. There are two deities with the same name.
* One of the {{ill|Katakurokushin|ja|家宅六神}}. It is considered to be the same deity as {{ill|Magatsuhi|lt=Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神}}. * An alternative name for {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神}}. '''Ōya-biko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. There are two deities with the same name.
* One of the {{ill|Katakurokushin|ja|家宅六神}}. It is considered to be the same deity as {{ill|Magatsuhi|lt=Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神}}. * An alternative name for {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神}}. '''Ōya-biko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. There are two deities with the same name.
* One of the {{ill|Katakurokushin|ja|家宅六神}}. It is considered to be the same deity as {{ill|Magatsuhi|lt=Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神}}. * An alternative name for {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神}}. '''Ōya-biko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. There are two deities with the same name.
* One of the {{ill|Katakurokushin|ja|家宅六神}}. It is considered to be the same deity as {{ill|Magatsuhi|lt=Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神}}. * An alternative name for {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神}}. revision.text:
'''Ōya-biko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. There are two deities with the same name.
* One of the {{ill|Katakurokushin|ja|家宅六神}}. It is considered to be the same deity as {{ill|Magatsuhi|lt=Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神}}. * An alternative name for {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神}}. '''Ōya-biko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. There are two deities with the same name.
* One of the {{ill|Katakurokushin|ja|家宅六神}}. It is considered to be the same deity as {{ill|Magatsuhi|lt=Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神}}. * An alternative name for {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神}}. revision.text:
'''Ōya-biko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. There are two deities with the same name.
* One of the {{ill|Katakurokushin|ja|家宅六神}}. It is considered to be the same deity as {{ill|Magatsuhi|lt=Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神}}. * An alternative name for {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神}}. '''Oyabiko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. There are two deities with the same name.
* One of the {{ill|Katakurokushin|ja|家宅六神}}. It is considered to be the same deity as {{ill|Magatsuhi|lt=Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神}}. * An alternative name for {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神}}. '''Oyabiko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. There are two deities with the same name.
* One of the {{ill|Katakurokushin|ja|家宅六神}}. It is considered to be the same deity as {{ill|Magatsuhi|lt=Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神}}. * An alternative name for {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神}}. '''Oyabiko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. There are two deities with the same name.
* One of the {{ill|Katakurokushin|ja|家宅六神}}. It is considered to be the same deity as {{ill|Magatsuhi|lt=Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神}}. * An alternative name for {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神}}. '''Oyabiko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. There are two deities with the same name.
* One of the {{ill|Katakurokushin|ja|家宅六神}}. It is considered to be the same deity as {{ill|Magatsuhi|lt=Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神}}. * An alternative name for {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神}}. '''Oyabiko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. There are two deities with the same name.
* One of the {{ill|Katakurokushin|ja|家宅六神}}. It is considered to be the same deity as {{ill|Magatsuhi|lt=Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神}}. * An alternative name for {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神}}. revision.text:
'''Oyabiko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. There are two deities with the same name.
* One of the {{ill|Katakurokushin|ja|家宅六神}}. It is considered to be the same deity as {{ill|Magatsuhi|lt=Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神}}. * An alternative name for {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神}}. [[ja:大屋毘古神]] [[ja: that appears in |ja: that appears in ]] {{aimai}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Oyabiko-no-Kami}}{{Shinto-stub}}# [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring revision.text:
'''Oyabiko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. There are two deities with the same name.
* One of the {{ill|Katakurokushin|ja|家宅六神}}. It is considered to be the same deity as {{ill|Magatsuhi|lt=Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神}}. * An alternative name for {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神}}. '''Oyabiko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. There are two deities with the same name.
* One of the {{ill|Katakurokushin|ja|家宅六神|12=draft}}. It is considered to be the same deity as {{ill|Magatsuhi|lt=Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神|12=draft}}. * An alternative name for {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神|12=draft}}.
{{aimai}}{{Shinto-stub}}#

'''Oyabiko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. There are two deities with the same name.
* One of the {{ill|Katakurokushin|ja|家宅六神|12=draft}}. It is considered to be the same deity as {{ill|Magatsuhi|lt=Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神|12=draft}}. * An alternative name for {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神|12=draft}}.

'''Oyabiko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. There are two deities with the same name.
* One of the {{ill|Katakurokushin|ja|家宅六神|12=draft}}. It is considered to be the same deity as {{ill|Magatsuhi|lt=Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神|12=draft}}. * An alternative name for {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神|12=draft}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大屋毘古神]][[zh:大屋毘古神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q10935046]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Oyabiko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. There are two deities with the same name.
* One of the {{ill|Katakurokushin|ja|家宅六神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakurokushin|qq=}}. It is considered to be the same deity as {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi|lt=Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神}}. * An alternative name for {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大屋毘古神]][[zh:大屋毘古神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大屋毘古神]][[zh:大屋毘古神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大屋毘古神]][[zh:大屋毘古神]]
'''Oyabiko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. There are two deities with the same name.
* One of the {{ill|Katakurokushin|ja|家宅六神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakurokushin|qq=}}. It is considered to be the same deity as {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi|lt=Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神}}. * An alternative name for {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.

'''Oyabiko-no-Kami''' (大屋毘古神) is a [[deity (Shinto)|deity]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. There are two deities with the same name.
* One of the {{ill|Katakurokushin|ja|家宅六神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakurokushin|qq=}}. It is considered to be the same deity as {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi|lt=Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神}}. * An alternative name for {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.
